titel: Follow-up of the national strategy to combat organized crime.
html: In this document, the government provides an update on the progress made in combating organized crime since the national strategy "Resilience and Action – A National Strategy Against Organized Crime" (report 2023/24:67) was submitted to the Parliament in February 2024. The government has actively and systematically worked to strengthen protection against crime, in line with the strategy's objectives of stopping criminal careers, reducing the availability of illegal weapons and explosives, disrupting the criminal economy, building resilience against undue influence, and ensuring reliable identities and effective information sharing. This work has been carried out both nationally and within the EU, as well as in other international forums. Overall, the government believes that Sweden's ability to actively combat organized crime has significantly improved and will continue to improve through the work being carried out within the framework of the strategy. However, the societal threat posed by the prevalence of organized crime remains. Therefore, it is important to continue working along the established path in a long-term and sustainable manner.
tldr: The government has released a report detailing the efforts to combat gangs and organized crime in Sweden. The report states that the work in this area has improved, but that crime remains a significant threat to society.
lattlast:
The government has published a report to demonstrate the progress of the national plan to combat organized crime. This plan was initially presented to the Parliament in February 2024. The plan's objective is to prevent criminals from continuing their activities and to reduce the number of illegal weapons and explosives in the country.

The work also focuses on disrupting the financial resources of criminals and protecting vital aspects of society from their influence. The government is working on this both within Sweden and in collaboration with other countries in the EU. Furthermore, the aim is to make it difficult to use false identities and to ensure that the police and authorities have access to accurate information.

The government believes that Sweden's ability to combat crime has significantly improved thanks to the ongoing efforts. They are confident that this will continue to improve in the future by maintaining the current strategy.

Despite the progress made, organized crime remains a significant threat to all residents of Sweden. Therefore, the government emphasizes the importance of continuing to work in the same way for an extended period to achieve the goals.
